summary: Split the mnruleplus address range out of the larger melee/mn/mn_2295.c menu object by adjusting GALE01 split boundaries, adding a new NonMatching build object, and refining symbol/data object boundaries around the split. The new src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c file is present as an empty placeholder in the diff excerpt, so this PR appears to be infrastructure for future decompilation rather than a source implementation change.
what_changed: Added new split entry melee/mn/mnruleplus.c with .text range 0x8023201C-0x80233A98.
Moved tail portions of mn_2295 data sections into mnruleplus: .data 0x803ED1D0-0x803ED340, .sdata 0x804D4B98-0x804D4BA0, .sbss 0x804D6BE0-0x804D6BE8, and .sdata2 0x804DBE38-0x804DBE70.
Added Object(NonMatching, "melee/mn/mnruleplus.c") to the mn Menus object list in configure.py.

Refined several broad data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt into smaller objects and explicit string/local symbols near 0x803EB958, 0x803EC83C, 0x803EC854, 0x803EC864, 0x803ED308, 0x803ED320, and 0x803ED330.
Added an empty src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c file according to the diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Introduced the new menu unit as NonMatching first, which preserves build integration while allowing the split to land before any functions are decompiled.
Aligned split boundaries across .text, .data, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 instead of only moving the text range, reducing the risk of cross-object section ownership mistakes.
Shrank overly large anonymous data objects and carved out string symbols, which should make later rodata/data matching and C declarations easier.
Kept the split adjacent to mnitemsw by ending mnruleplus at the existing mnitemsw start address 0x80233A98 / 0x803ED340.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a source object out of a larger nonmatching unit, update all relevant section ranges in splits.txt, not just .text.
Add the new source file to configure.py immediately as a NonMatching object so the build graph reflects the new split even before source code is implemented.

Large data blobs around a split boundary should be subdivided into accurate object/string symbols before or during the split; otherwise ownership and generated declarations become harder to reason about.
Empty placeholder C files can be useful for establishing object boundaries and build wiring as a standalone PR.
naming_conventions: The new source unit is named melee/mn/mnruleplus.c, matching the PR title and the menu subsystem path.
Existing unknown menu symbols keep the mn_ address-based naming style, for example mn_803ED1D0 and mn_803ED308.
Local jump tables retain the jumptable_ address naming convention, such as jumptable_803EB9B0.
The neighboring item switch symbols use the existing mnItemSw_ prefix capitalization, for example mnItemSw_803ED340.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used split-range surgery to isolate functions from 0x8023201C through 0x80233A98 into a separate object.
Kept mnruleplus marked NonMatching, indicating this PR did not attempt function-level matching yet.

Adjusted data symbol sizes to make section boundaries line up with the new object split, including reducing mn_803EB6B0 from 0x300 to 0x2A8, mn_803EC818 from 0x9B8 to 0x24, and mn_803ED2E8 from 0x58 to 0x20.
Identified string data explicitly in symbols.txt using data:string annotations near the split.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No human PR body text was provided in the context; interpretation is based on title, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that implement or match functions in src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c.
Check whether mnruleplus.c remains NonMatching or was later moved to Matching in configure.py.
Search for later renames of mn_803ED308, mn_803ED320, and mn_803ED330 into meaningful string/global names.
Review subsequent menu subsystem splits around mn_2295.c and mnitemsw.c for the same split-boundary pattern.
